item.title: FGV sofre vazamento de 1,52 TB em suposto ataque cibernético do grupo Dragonforce - TecMundo
item.description: FGV (Fundação Getulio Vargas), a major Brazilian educational and research institution, experienced a significant data breach involving the leak of 1.52 TB of data. The attack has been attributed to the Dragonforce hacking group. The incident represents a substantial security compromise affecting the organization's sensitive information and systems.

item.title: AI-Driven Cyber Threats Rise Across Mexico, Latin America - Mexico Business News
item.description: The report highlights an increasing trend of AI-driven cyber threats targeting Mexico and Latin America, indicating a shift in attack sophistication and methodology. These threats leverage artificial intelligence to enhance attack effectiveness, targeting both public and private sector organizations across the region. The rising prevalence of AI-powered attacks suggests threat actors are adopting more advanced techniques to evade detection and maximize impact on critical infrastructure and business operations.

item.title: Guerra abre ventana inmediata de ataques cibernéticos - Publimetro México
item.description: The article discusses how armed conflicts create immediate opportunities for cyberattacks against critical infrastructure and government systems. During wartime, threat actors exploit the chaos and reduced cybersecurity resources to launch attacks on vulnerable targets. This represents a significant cybersecurity risk as nations engaged in conflict face heightened exposure to both state-sponsored and opportunistic cyber threats.

item.title: La IA acelera los ciberataques y presiona la seguridad de la infraestructura crítica en México - El Economista
item.description: The article discusses how artificial intelligence is being leveraged to accelerate cyberattacks targeting Mexico's critical infrastructure, creating significant security challenges. AI-powered attacks are enabling threat actors to conduct more sophisticated and faster assaults on essential systems, putting national infrastructure at increased risk. The report highlights the growing pressure on Mexico's cybersecurity defenses as attackers utilize machine learning and automation to identify vulnerabilities and execute attacks more efficiently.

item.title: México concentra 30% de ciberataques en Latinoamérica: OEA - Buzos de la noticia
item.description: According to the Organization of American States (OAS), Mexico accounts for 30% of all cyberattacks in Latin America, highlighting the region's significant cybersecurity challenges. This threat intelligence report underscores Mexico's vulnerability to cyber threats and its disproportionate exposure compared to other Latin American nations. The data suggests that Mexico faces elevated risks from various threat actors targeting critical infrastructure, businesses, and government entities across the country.

item.title: México, uno de los países con más ciberataques en Latinoamérica - WIRED
VULNERABLES ANTE UN FUTURO CIBERATAQUE - unicobc.com.mx
item.description: The article discusses vulnerabilities that could expose organizations to future cyberattacks. It appears to be a threat intelligence or security awareness piece highlighting the risks and preparedness gaps that organizations face in their cybersecurity posture. The content emphasizes the importance of identifying and addressing vulnerabilities before they can be exploited by threat actors.

item.title: ¿Cuántas filtraciones más necesita el sector educativo mexicano para reaccionar ante los ciberataques? - Infobae
item.description: This article discusses the recurring problem of data breaches affecting Mexico's education sector, questioning why the sector continues to experience multiple security incidents without implementing adequate protective measures. The piece highlights the vulnerability of educational institutions to cyberattacks and the lack of sufficient response mechanisms to prevent future breaches. It emphasizes the need for the education sector to take cybersecurity threats more seriously and implement comprehensive security protocols.

item.title: Prevén alza de ciberataques por guerra - El Sol de México
悪意あるアプリが「4200万回DL」の衝撃…拡大するモバイル脅威、2026年「7つの予測」 ｜Seizo Trend - ビジネス+IT
item.description: The article discusses a malicious mobile application that achieved 42 million downloads, highlighting the growing threat landscape for mobile devices. It presents seven predictions for mobile security threats expected to emerge or escalate by 2026, indicating an expanding attack surface in the mobile ecosystem. The report emphasizes the increasing sophistication and scale of mobile-based cyber threats targeting users globally.

item.title: イラン軍事作戦 サイバー・宇宙空間での攻撃が大きな効果か - NHKニュース
item.description: Iranian military operations have reportedly demonstrated significant effectiveness through cyberattacks targeting cyber and space domains. This represents nation-state level cyber warfare capabilities being deployed as part of broader military strategy. The incident highlights Iran's growing sophistication in conducting coordinated cyber operations alongside traditional military activities.

item.title: ランサムウエア攻撃、身代金交渉の「コモディティー化」進む＝専門家 - Reuters
item.description: Security experts report that ransomware attacks are experiencing increased 'commoditization' of ransom negotiations, indicating a shift in how cybercriminals conduct extortion operations. This trend suggests that ransom negotiation tactics and processes are becoming standardized and more widely accessible across criminal groups. The development reflects the maturation of ransomware-as-a-service (RaaS) ecosystems and the professionalization of the ransomware threat landscape.

item.title: ＜1分で解説＞サイバー攻撃で水道使用者の個人情報漏えいか 東京 - au Webポータル
item.description: A cyberattack has potentially resulted in the leakage of personal information belonging to water utility users in Tokyo through the au Web Portal. The incident involves unauthorized access to customer data, raising concerns about the security of critical infrastructure and utility service providers. This breach highlights vulnerabilities in systems managing essential services and customer personal information.

item.title: 米軍、イラン軍事作戦に｢サイバー攻撃と数万発爆撃｣ 追加部隊を派遣 - 日本経済新聞
item.description: The U.S. military has reportedly conducted cyberattacks against Iranian military operations in conjunction with conventional military strikes involving tens of thousands of airstrikes. The U.S. is deploying additional troops to the region as part of this coordinated military response. This represents a significant nation-state cyber and kinetic operation targeting Iranian military infrastructure and capabilities.

item.title: 北朝鮮関連ハッカー、仮想通貨企業を標的に大規模サイバー攻撃か＝レポート - Iolite（アイオライト）
item.description: According to a report, hackers linked to North Korea have allegedly conducted a large-scale cyberattack targeting cryptocurrency companies. This incident aligns with known patterns of North Korean threat actors targeting the cryptocurrency sector for financial gain and sanctions evasion. The attack underscores the ongoing threat posed by nation-state actors to digital asset platforms and financial infrastructure.

item.title: イラン関連のサイバー攻撃活発化か ロシア系ハクティビストの活動も パロアルト調査 - ITmedia
item.description: According to a Palo Alto Networks investigation, there are indications of increased cyberattack activity linked to Iran, alongside notable activity from Russian-affiliated hacktivists. The report suggests a potential escalation in coordinated cyber operations from these threat actors. This threat intelligence finding highlights ongoing geopolitical tensions manifesting through increased cyber operations targeting various entities.

item.title: IoT機器の安全性を可視化する｢JC-STAR｣ サイバー攻撃から企業を守る､最低限の基準 - 東洋経済オンライン
item.description: The article discusses JC-STAR, a framework for visualizing and assessing the security posture of IoT devices in enterprise environments. It establishes minimum security standards designed to protect organizations from cyberattacks targeting IoT infrastructure. The framework helps companies identify vulnerabilities and security gaps in their connected devices, which are increasingly targeted by threat actors.

item.title: AIエージェントの相互作用が招くシステム破壊やサイバー攻撃の“増幅” - ZDNET Japan
item.description: This article discusses how interactions between AI agents can amplify and escalate system destruction and cyberattack capabilities. The report highlights emerging security risks associated with autonomous AI systems operating in coordination, potentially creating cascading failures or enhanced attack vectors. This represents a significant threat intelligence concern regarding the evolving landscape of AI-driven cybersecurity threats.

item.title: 「JC-STAR」制度とは？ IoT製品のセキュリティ水準を証明できるビジネスメリットとは？ - ASCII.jp
item.description: The article discusses the JC-STAR certification system, a Japanese security standard framework designed to verify and certify the security levels of IoT products. JC-STAR provides businesses with a standardized way to demonstrate their IoT devices meet established security requirements, offering competitive advantages in the market. The system addresses growing concerns about IoT security vulnerabilities and helps manufacturers establish trust with consumers and enterprise customers.

item.title: アドバンテスト、生産・出荷「問題なし」＝サイバー攻撃、情報流出は確認中(時事通信) - Yahoo!ファイナンス
item.description: Advantest, a major semiconductor testing equipment manufacturer, has confirmed it is investigating a cyberattack and potential information leakage. The company reports that production and shipment operations are currently functioning normally without disruption. The investigation is ongoing to determine the scope and nature of any data that may have been compromised.

item.title: サイバー兵士が「主要戦力」に昇格 ～ 米国防総省がサイバー攻撃実施を公然と認める - ScanNetSecurity
item.description: The U.S. Department of Defense has publicly acknowledged conducting cyber attacks and elevated cyber soldiers to a primary military capability. This represents a significant shift in military doctrine, positioning cyber operations as a core strategic asset alongside traditional military forces. The announcement reflects the growing importance of cyber warfare in modern defense strategies and potential escalation in state-sponsored cyber operations.

item.title: アドバンテスト、サイバー攻撃の影響は「ほぼ解消」（EE Times Japan） - Yahoo!ニュース
item.description: Advantest, a major semiconductor testing equipment manufacturer, has reported that the impact of a cyberattack on its operations has been nearly resolved. The company experienced a significant security incident that disrupted its business operations, but recovery efforts have progressed substantially. This incident highlights the vulnerability of critical semiconductor supply chain companies to cyber threats.

item.title: サイバー攻撃で患者情報漏洩、医療企業が1,100万ドル支払い - abc株式会社
item.description: A healthcare company suffered a cyberattack resulting in the exposure of patient information, leading to a settlement payment of $11 million. The incident highlights the significant financial and reputational impact of data breaches in the healthcare sector. This case underscores the critical importance of robust cybersecurity measures to protect sensitive personal health information.

item.title: スパイと犯罪組織に渡ったiPhone攻撃ツール、起源は米政府向けの可能性 - WIRED.jp
item.description: A sophisticated iPhone attack tool has been discovered being used by spies and criminal organizations, with evidence suggesting its origins may trace back to U.S. government development. The tool represents a significant security threat as it has proliferated beyond its intended use to malicious actors. This incident highlights concerns about the proliferation of government-developed cyber weapons and their potential misuse by threat actors.

item.title: イラン指導部の行動を丸裸にした｢サイバー作戦｣の中身､AI･データ･アルゴリズムが一体となった"知能化戦争"の幕開け - 東洋経済オンライン
item.description: This article discusses sophisticated cyber operations targeting Iranian leadership that exposed their activities and communications. The operations represent an evolution in cyber warfare tactics, integrating artificial intelligence, data analytics, and algorithmic techniques to conduct intelligence gathering and surveillance. The incident exemplifies the emergence of advanced, AI-driven cyber espionage capabilities in modern geopolitical conflicts.

item.title: 米国が史上最大規模のサイバー攻撃、ネットを全て遮断されたイランは「デジタル・ブラックアウト」状態（朝鮮日報日本語版） - Yahoo!ニュース
item.description: The United States has reportedly conducted a massive cyberattack against Iran, resulting in a widespread internet outage described as a 'digital blackout' across the country. The attack represents one of the largest cyberattacks in history and has effectively severed Iran's connectivity to the internet. This incident reflects escalating cyber warfare between the two nations and demonstrates the capability to cause significant disruption to critical infrastructure at a national scale.

item.title: アドバンテスト－買い気配 サイバー攻撃に関する続報公表 サービス提供支障なし(トレーダーズ・ウェブ) - Yahoo!ファイナンス
item.description: Advantest, a major semiconductor testing equipment manufacturer, has published a follow-up report regarding a cyberattack incident. The company confirmed that despite the security incident, there has been no disruption to service provision for customers. The stock showed buying interest following the disclosure of the incident details.

item.title: サイバー攻撃対策の強化呼びかけ ホワイトハッカーが最新のサイバー攻撃事情紹介（2026年3月5日掲載）｜日テレNEWS NNN - 日テレNEWS NNN
item.description: White hat hackers have presented an overview of current cybersecurity threats and attack trends, emphasizing the need for enhanced defensive measures. The report highlights emerging cyber attack methodologies and vulnerabilities that organizations should be aware of. This threat intelligence briefing aims to inform the public and businesses about contemporary cybersecurity risks and best practices for protection.

item.title: 米金融業界、イラン戦争激化でサイバー攻撃への警戒強化 - Reuters
item.description: The US financial industry is heightening its cybersecurity defenses in response to escalating tensions with Iran, which has historically conducted cyberattacks against American financial institutions. The alert reflects concerns that geopolitical conflicts could trigger increased cyber operations targeting critical financial infrastructure. Financial institutions are implementing enhanced monitoring and defensive measures to protect against potential Iranian state-sponsored or affiliated cyber threats.

item.title: サイバー攻撃、最後の砦は「人」 10代も活躍へ 防衛人材の裾野拡大 - 日経ビジネス電子版
item.description: This article discusses Japan's approach to cybersecurity defense, emphasizing the importance of human expertise as the critical final defense against cyberattacks. The piece highlights efforts to expand the talent pool for cybersecurity professionals by engaging younger generations, including teenagers, in defense roles. This reflects a strategic shift toward building a broader cybersecurity workforce to address growing threats to critical infrastructure and national security.

item.title: 静岡県の白梅豊岡病院 ランサムウェアによるサイバー攻撃で個人情報漏洩の恐れ-ハッカーが不正アクセスを主張 - 合同会社ロケットボーイズ
item.description: Hakubai Toyooka Hospital in Shizuoka Prefecture, Japan, has fallen victim to a ransomware cyberattack with hackers claiming unauthorized access to the hospital's systems. The attack poses a significant risk of personal information leakage, potentially exposing patient data and sensitive medical records. This incident highlights the ongoing threat of ransomware attacks targeting healthcare facilities, which are critical infrastructure organizations handling sensitive personal and medical information.

item.title: Nike(ナイキ)がサイバー攻撃で個人情報漏洩の恐れ-日本ユーザーへセキュリティインシデントを通知 - 合同会社ロケットボーイズ
item.description: Nike has notified Japanese users of a security incident involving a cyberattack that resulted in potential personal information exposure. The company has informed affected users about the data breach and is taking measures to address the incident. This represents a significant data breach affecting Nike's Japanese customer base with potential exposure of personal and account information.

item.title: 武蔵小杉病院へのサイバー攻撃､ナースコールに障害･･･企業とはまた違う"命の問題"になりうる｢病院が標的｣の深刻度 - 東洋経済オンライン
item.description: Musashi-Kosugi Hospital experienced a cyberattack that disrupted its nurse call system, highlighting the critical severity of healthcare infrastructure being targeted by cybercriminals. Unlike corporate attacks that primarily cause financial or operational damage, attacks on hospitals pose direct threats to patient safety and medical care delivery. The incident underscores the heightened risk and life-threatening consequences when hospitals become targets of cyberattacks.

item.title: イラン攻撃に関連したサイバー攻撃が急増、セキュリティー企業が相次ぎ報告 - 日経クロステック
item.description: Security companies are reporting a sharp increase in cyberattacks linked to the Iran situation. The incidents appear to be coordinated nation-state level operations, with multiple security firms documenting the activity. This represents a significant escalation in cyber operations related to geopolitical tensions in the Middle East.

item.title: GMOインターネットにみる最新SIEMの威力、5つの必須機能と7つの活用戦略とは - 日経クロステック Active
Microsoft: ハッカーは AI を利用してフィッシングメールを作成し、マルウェアを開発し、世界的なサイバー攻撃を加速させています。 - Vietnam.vn
item.description: Microsoft reports that threat actors are leveraging artificial intelligence to enhance their cyberattack capabilities, including the creation of sophisticated phishing emails and malware development. This represents a significant escalation in cyber threats globally, as attackers combine AI tools with traditional attack vectors to increase effectiveness and scale. The report highlights how AI is being weaponized to automate and improve various stages of cyberattacks, from initial compromise through malware deployment.

item.title: 既知の脆弱性を悪用しサーバに侵入 ～ ブロードバンドタワーにサイバー攻撃 - ScanNetSecurity
item.description: Broadband Tower experienced a cyberattack where threat actors exploited a known vulnerability to gain unauthorized access to their servers. The attack demonstrates the continued risk posed by unpatched systems and known security flaws. This incident highlights the importance of timely vulnerability patching and security updates in preventing server compromises.

item.title: アドバンテスト「中核業務は問題なく稼働」 サイバー攻撃受け - 日本経済新聞
item.description: Advantest, a major semiconductor testing equipment manufacturer, has confirmed it experienced a cyberattack but stated that its core business operations continue to function normally. The company is investigating the extent of the attack and its impact on systems and data. This incident highlights the ongoing threat to critical technology infrastructure and supply chain vulnerabilities in the semiconductor industry.

item.title: ＪＡあきた北ライフサービス、サイバー攻撃で個人情報漏洩の恐れ ランサムウェアグループ INCランサムウェアが犯行声明 - 合同会社ロケットボーイズ
item.description: JA Akita Kita Life Service suffered a ransomware attack by the INC ransomware group, which has claimed responsibility for the incident. The attack resulted in potential exposure of personal information belonging to customers and members of the agricultural cooperative. This represents a significant data breach affecting a Japanese financial services organization serving the Akita region.

item.title: 「ハッカーらはサイバー攻撃の各段階でAIを悪用」マイクロソフトが報告 - 株式会社マキナレコード
item.description: Microsoft has released a threat intelligence report detailing how hackers are leveraging artificial intelligence throughout all phases of cyberattacks, from reconnaissance and initial access to lateral movement and data exfiltration. The report highlights the evolving threat landscape where adversaries are increasingly automating and enhancing their attack capabilities using AI tools. This finding underscores the growing sophistication of cyber threats and the need for organizations to strengthen their defensive measures against AI-augmented attacks.

item.title: 東京都水道局の事業を再委託された会社がサイバー攻撃受ける およそ13万件の個人情報が流出のおそれ - Yahoo!ニュース
item.description: A company subcontracted by the Tokyo Metropolitan Waterworks Bureau experienced a cyberattack resulting in the potential exposure of approximately 130,000 personal information records. The incident affects individuals whose data was handled by the subcontracted company in connection with Tokyo's water utility operations. This represents a significant data breach involving critical infrastructure services and personal customer information.

item.title: 「もらい事故でした、申し訳ありません」では済まない、“他社”で発生したサイバー攻撃による被害の厳しい現実 - Yahoo!ニュース
item.description: This article discusses the serious consequences and liability issues that arise when companies suffer damage from cyberattacks originating at third-party or partner organizations. It highlights that organizations cannot simply dismiss such incidents as unavoidable accidents, as they face significant business impact and potential legal responsibility. The piece emphasizes the growing threat of supply chain-based cyberattacks and the need for companies to implement robust security measures across their entire ecosystem, not just within their own infrastructure.

item.title: サイバー攻撃で個人情報流出か (2026年3月7日掲載) - ライブドアニュース
フィッシングでEV証明書付き偽アプリを配布、Microsoftが新攻撃を確認 - マイナビニュース
item.description: Microsoft has identified a new phishing campaign distributing fraudulent applications equipped with EV (Extended Validation) certificates to deceive users into believing they are legitimate. Attackers are leveraging the trust associated with EV certificates to increase the credibility of malicious apps and bypass security warnings. This sophisticated approach combines phishing tactics with certificate spoofing to compromise user systems and potentially steal credentials or sensitive data.

item.title: 東京都の再委託事業者がサイバー攻撃被害、最大１３万世帯の情報漏えい恐れ…水道使用者名や使用水量 - 読売新聞オンライン
item.description: A subcontractor handling operations for the Tokyo Metropolitan Government experienced a cyberattack resulting in potential data exposure affecting up to 130,000 households. The compromised information includes water users' names and water usage amounts. This incident highlights vulnerabilities in the supply chain of critical infrastructure services and the risks posed to personal data of utility customers.

item.title: 北朝鮮関連ハッカー、仮想通貨企業を標的に大規模サイバー攻撃か＝レポート - CoinPost
item.description: According to a report, hackers linked to North Korea have allegedly conducted a large-scale cyberattack targeting cryptocurrency companies. This incident reflects ongoing nation-state cyber threats against the cryptocurrency sector, which has become a prime target for state-sponsored actors seeking to acquire digital assets and fund illicit activities. The attack demonstrates the persistent threat posed by North Korean threat actors to financial and cryptocurrency infrastructure.

item.title: 1分で解説＞サイバー攻撃で水道使用者の個人情報漏えいか 東京（毎日新聞） - Yahoo!ニュース
item.description: A cyberattack has potentially resulted in the leakage of personal information belonging to water utility users in Tokyo. The incident appears to have compromised sensitive data of customers using the water service. This represents a significant data breach affecting critical infrastructure and civilian users in the Tokyo area.

item.title: あなたの家電がサイバー攻撃の踏み台に？ 購入時は星マークをチェックしよう：IoTセキュリティ - MONOist
item.description: This article discusses IoT security risks related to home appliances being compromised and used as launching points for cyberattacks. It provides guidance for consumers to check security certifications (indicated by star marks) when purchasing IoT devices to mitigate the risk of their appliances being exploited by attackers. The piece emphasizes the importance of selecting secure IoT products to prevent devices from becoming part of botnets or attack infrastructure.

item.title: ｢もらい事故でした､申し訳ありません｣では済まない､"他社"で発生したサイバー攻撃による被害の厳しい現実 - 東洋経済オンライン
item.description: This article discusses the serious consequences and liability issues that arise when companies suffer cyberattack damage originating from third-party vendors or supply chain partners. It highlights that organizations cannot simply dismiss such incidents as collateral damage, as they face significant financial and reputational impacts. The piece emphasizes the need for robust supply chain security measures and vendor risk management to mitigate exposure to attacks originating outside one's own infrastructure.

item.title: ‘Indian firms embrace AI, increase spending to counter emerging cyber threats’ - CXOToday.com
Over 135,000 ransomware attacks detected in Southeast Asia in 2024 - MSN
AI-driven cyberattacks surge in Asia-Pacific, IBM warns - DataCenterNews Asia Pacific
item.description: IBM has issued a warning about a significant increase in AI-driven cyberattacks targeting the Asia-Pacific region. The report highlights how threat actors are leveraging artificial intelligence to enhance the sophistication and effectiveness of their attacks. This threat intelligence indicates a concerning trend of attackers using AI to automate and improve various cyber attack methodologies across the region.

item.title: الحرب التي لا تُرى.. الصراع السيبراني بين أمريكا وإسرائيل وإيران من مفاعل نطنز إلى مراكز البيانات في الخليج - المصدر أونلاين
item.description: This article discusses the ongoing cyber warfare between the United States, Israel, and Iran, highlighting significant incidents including the Natanz nuclear facility attack and operations targeting data centers in the Gulf region. The report covers nation-state cyber operations and their strategic implications in the Middle East, demonstrating the escalating sophistication of state-sponsored cyber attacks on critical infrastructure. The conflict represents a broader pattern of cyber espionage and sabotage operations conducted by major powers in the region.

item.title: مركز الأمن السيبراني ينفي استهدف منظومة الكهرباء بهجوم سيبراني - الواجهة - المربد
item.description: The cybersecurity center has issued a denial regarding allegations that the electrical power system was targeted in a cyberattack. This statement addresses concerns about potential threats to critical infrastructure. The denial suggests there were reports or claims of a cyberattack against the power grid that the official cybersecurity authority is refuting.

item.title: اختراق ضخم في المكسيك.. قراصنة يستغلون الذكاء الاصطناعي لسرقة آلاف البيانات - Elfann
item.description: A significant data breach has occurred in Mexico where cybercriminals have exploited artificial intelligence technologies to steal thousands of data records. The attackers leveraged AI capabilities to automate and scale their data theft operations, targeting sensitive information at scale. This incident highlights the emerging threat of AI-enabled cyberattacks and the vulnerability of organizations to sophisticated, automated breach techniques.

item.title: وكالة الإقتصاد نيوز - مركز الأمن السيبراني ينفي استهداف الكهرباء بهجوم سيبراني - economy-news.net
item.description: The Cybersecurity Center has issued a denial regarding reports of a cyberattack targeting the electricity sector. This statement addresses concerns about potential critical infrastructure threats to power systems. The denial suggests there were allegations or rumors of a cyberattack on electrical infrastructure that the security center has refuted.

item.title: الأمن السيبراني في العراق ينفي تعرض منظومة الكهرباء لهجوم إلكتروني - Kurdistan24
item.description: Iraqi cybersecurity authorities have denied reports of a cyberattack targeting the country's electrical power system. The denial suggests there were allegations or concerns about potential attacks on critical infrastructure. This statement is significant given Iraq's history of vulnerability to cyberattacks targeting essential services and critical infrastructure.

item.title: مسؤول لـ«العين الإخبارية»: هجوم سيبراني إسرائيلي وراء انقطاع الكهرباء بالعراق - العين الإخبارية
item.description: An official has attributed a widespread power outage in Iraq to an Israeli cyberattack, indicating a critical infrastructure attack targeting the country's electrical grid. This incident represents a nation-state level cyber operation against essential infrastructure, potentially affecting millions of civilians. The attack highlights the vulnerability of Iraq's power systems to sophisticated cyber threats and raises concerns about escalating cyber warfare in the region.

item.title: الأردن يواجه الهجمات السيبرانية خلف غبار الصواريخ - اندبندنت عربية
item.description: Jordan is reportedly experiencing cyberattacks occurring alongside or in conjunction with missile-related incidents. The article suggests a coordinated or opportunistic cyber campaign targeting the country during a period of heightened military tension. This represents a multi-vector threat combining kinetic and cyber operations against critical infrastructure or government systems.

item.title: Phishing Campaign Uses Google Cloud to Host Malicious Redirects via GCS Bucket - gbhackers.com

Attackers are using this technique to redirect victims to phishing pages or malware distribution sites while appearing to originate from a trusted cloud provider. This method is particularly effective because security filters often whitelist Google Cloud domains, making it difficult for traditional email and web security solutions to block the malicious content.

item.title: Threat actors weaponize OAuth redirection logic to deliver malware - Help Net Security
item.description: Threat actors are exploiting OAuth redirection mechanisms to deliver malware to unsuspecting users. The technique represents a sophisticated abuse of legitimate authentication protocols, making it difficult for users and security systems to detect the malicious activity.

item.title: Microsoft warns of new signed malware which deploys remote monitoring tools as backdoors - TechRadar

item.title: New phishing attacks exploit stolen digital certificates for malicious software - SC Media

item.title: Fake Google Security page used in PWA phishing campaign - SC Media

item.title: Cisco Warns of More Catalyst SD-WAN Flaws Exploited in the Wild - SecurityWeek
item.description: Cisco has disclosed additional vulnerabilities in its Catalyst SD-WAN products that are being actively exploited by threat actors in the wild. These flaws pose a significant risk to organizations using Cisco's SD-WAN solutions for network management and connectivity. The company has issued warnings urging customers to apply patches and implement mitigations to prevent unauthorized access and potential network compromise.

item.title: Possible U.S.-developed exploits linked to first known ‘mass’ iOS attack - CyberScoop
item.description: Security researchers have identified a significant iOS attack campaign potentially involving U.S.-developed exploits, marking the first known large-scale attack targeting Apple's iOS platform. The incident suggests sophisticated threat actors may have access to previously unknown vulnerabilities in iOS. This discovery raises concerns about the proliferation of exploit tools and their potential use in widespread mobile device compromises.

item.title: Google’s Biggest Android Security Update in Years Fixes 129 Bugs, Including an Actively Exploited Zero-Day - TechRepublic
item.description: Google released a major Android security update addressing 129 vulnerabilities, including a zero-day vulnerability that was actively being exploited in the wild. This comprehensive patch represents one of the largest security updates for Android in recent years, aimed at protecting millions of devices from ongoing threats. The update prioritizes critical security fixes to mitigate active exploitation risks and improve overall platform security.

item.title: Enterprise Tech Facing Record Zero‑Day Pressure, Finds Google - digit.fyi
item.description: Google has reported that enterprise technology is experiencing unprecedented pressure from zero-day vulnerabilities, indicating a significant increase in exploitation of previously unknown security flaws. This finding highlights the growing threat landscape where attackers are actively discovering and leveraging zero-day exploits against enterprise systems before vendors can develop patches. The report underscores the critical need for organizations to strengthen their vulnerability management and threat detection capabilities to defend against these unpatched security threats.

item.title: Microsoft top company targeted with zero days in 2025, followed by Google and Apple: Full list here - The Times of India
item.description: Microsoft has become the most targeted company for zero-day exploits in 2025, followed by Google and Apple. The article highlights a concerning trend where major technology companies are increasingly being targeted with previously unknown vulnerabilities. This reflects the growing sophistication of threat actors and the critical need for enhanced security measures across the tech industry.

item.title: PoC Exploit Released for Cisco SD-WAN 0-Day Vulnerability Actively Exploited in the Wild - cyberpress.org
item.description: A proof-of-concept exploit has been released for a previously unknown zero-day vulnerability in Cisco SD-WAN that is currently being actively exploited by threat actors in the wild. This disclosure increases the risk to organizations using affected Cisco SD-WAN deployments, as the availability of public exploit code enables broader attack campaigns. The vulnerability poses a significant threat to enterprise network infrastructure, particularly for organizations relying on SD-WAN for critical connectivity and security operations.

item.title: Update Your Android ASAP to Patch These 129 Security Flaws - Lifehacker
item.description: Google has released a critical security update for Android addressing 129 security vulnerabilities that require immediate patching. The flaws span multiple severity levels and could potentially be exploited by attackers to compromise device security and user data. Users are strongly advised to update their Android devices as soon as possible to protect against potential exploitation of these vulnerabilities.

item.title: CSVs led attributed zero-day exploitation as enterprise targets rose, Google says - TechInformed
item.description: Google reports that Chrome Security Vulnerabilities (CSVs) have been increasingly exploited in zero-day attacks targeting enterprise environments. The analysis indicates a rising trend in zero-day exploitation campaigns focused on business organizations. This threat intelligence highlights the growing risk enterprises face from unpatched critical vulnerabilities being actively weaponized by threat actors.

item.title: Feds take notice of iOS vulnerabilities exploited under mysterious circumstances - Ars Technica
item.description: Federal authorities have become aware of iOS vulnerabilities that are being actively exploited in undisclosed circumstances. The mysterious nature of the exploitation suggests potential sophisticated threat actors targeting Apple's mobile platform. This incident highlights the critical need for rapid patching and investigation into how these vulnerabilities are being weaponized in the wild.

item.title: Google GTIG: 90 zero-day flaws exploited in 2025 as enterprise targets grow - Security Affairs
item.description: Google's Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG) reported that 90 zero-day vulnerabilities were actively exploited in 2025, with an increasing focus on enterprise targets. This significant rise in zero-day exploitation demonstrates a growing threat landscape where attackers are leveraging previously unknown vulnerabilities to compromise organizations. The trend indicates that enterprises face heightened risk from sophisticated threat actors who are investing in discovering and weaponizing zero-day flaws for targeted attacks.

item.title: Recent Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Vulnerability Now Widely Exploited - SecurityWeek
item.description: A vulnerability in Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN has been discovered and is now being actively exploited in the wild. The vulnerability affects Cisco's software-defined wide area network (SD-WAN) platform, which is widely deployed in enterprise networks. This widespread exploitation poses a significant risk to organizations using this technology, as attackers can leverage the flaw to compromise network infrastructure and potentially gain unauthorized access to sensitive systems.

item.title: Google: Half of 2025’s 90 Exploited Zero-Days Aimed at Enterprises - SecurityWeek
item.description: Google reported that approximately 45 of the 90 zero-day vulnerabilities exploited in 2025 were specifically targeted at enterprise systems, indicating a significant shift in attacker focus toward organizational targets. This finding highlights the growing sophistication and prevalence of zero-day exploits being weaponized against businesses rather than individual users. The data underscores the critical need for enterprises to implement robust vulnerability management and threat detection strategies to defend against these previously unknown security flaws.

item.title: Actively exploited zero-days rise as surveillance firms ramp up abuse - SC Media
item.description: The article reports on an increasing trend of zero-day vulnerabilities being actively exploited in the wild, with surveillance firms contributing to the abuse of these vulnerabilities. This represents a significant cybersecurity threat as zero-days are previously unknown vulnerabilities with no available patches, making systems vulnerable to exploitation. The involvement of surveillance firms in weaponizing these exploits raises concerns about both criminal and state-sponsored cyber threats targeting organizations and individuals.

item.title: Android Update Patches Exploited Qualcomm Zero-Day - SecurityWeek
item.description: Google has released an Android security update addressing a previously exploited zero-day vulnerability in Qualcomm chipsets. The vulnerability was actively being exploited in the wild before the patch was made available. This update is critical for Android users as it closes a significant security gap that could allow attackers to gain elevated privileges or execute arbitrary code on affected devices.

item.title: Google Reports 90 Zero-Day Vulnerabilities Exploited by Hackers in 2025 - cyberpress.org
item.description: Google has disclosed that 90 zero-day vulnerabilities were actively exploited by hackers during 2025, representing a significant increase in zero-day exploitation activity. These previously unknown vulnerabilities were leveraged by threat actors before patches could be developed and deployed, posing serious risks to users and organizations. The disclosure highlights the growing sophistication of attackers and the critical importance of rapid vulnerability patching and threat intelligence sharing across the industry.

item.title: Google Threat Intelligence Group warns enterprise systems increasingly targeted by zero-day exploits - SiliconANGLE
item.description: Google's Threat Intelligence Group has issued a warning that enterprise systems are facing an increasing number of zero-day exploit attacks. The alert highlights a growing trend where threat actors are actively targeting vulnerabilities in business infrastructure that have not yet been publicly disclosed or patched. This represents a significant security concern for organizations as zero-day exploits cannot be mitigated through standard patching procedures until the vulnerability is discovered and addressed.

item.title: Google warns of surge in enterprise zero-day attacks - SecurityBrief Australia
item.description: Google has issued a warning about a significant increase in zero-day attacks targeting enterprise environments. These attacks exploit previously unknown vulnerabilities before vendors can develop and deploy patches, making them particularly dangerous. The surge represents a growing threat to organizations as attackers increasingly leverage unpatched security flaws to gain unauthorized access to corporate systems and sensitive data.

item.title: Organizations hit by 90 zero-day vulnerabilities last year - ITPro
item.description: Organizations faced significant security threats from 90 zero-day vulnerabilities discovered in the previous year, according to ITPro reporting. Zero-day vulnerabilities represent critical security risks as they are unknown to vendors and have no available patches, leaving systems exposed to exploitation. This threat intelligence highlights the ongoing challenge organizations face in defending against previously unknown attack vectors and the importance of robust vulnerability management and threat detection strategies.

item.title: Nearly half of exploited zero-day flaws target enterprise-grade technology - Cybersecurity Dive
item.description: A cybersecurity analysis reveals that approximately 50% of actively exploited zero-day vulnerabilities are targeting enterprise-grade technology and software. This finding highlights a significant threat to organizations, as zero-day flaws lack available patches and are particularly valuable to threat actors. The trend underscores the need for enterprises to implement robust detection and response capabilities to mitigate risks from unpatched vulnerabilities.

item.title: Google addresses actively exploited Qualcomm zero-day in fresh batch of 129 Android vulnerabilities - CyberScoop
item.description: Google has released patches for 129 Android vulnerabilities, including an actively exploited zero-day flaw in Qualcomm chipsets. The vulnerability poses a significant risk to Android devices as it is currently being leveraged in real-world attacks. This security update addresses critical gaps in the Android ecosystem and aims to protect users from ongoing exploitation campaigns.

item.title: Google Confirms 90 Zero-Day Vulnerabilities Actively Exploited in 2025 - CybersecurityNews
item.description: Google has confirmed that 90 zero-day vulnerabilities were actively exploited in 2025, representing a significant threat landscape. These unpatched vulnerabilities were being leveraged by threat actors in real-world attacks before fixes were available. The disclosure highlights the critical need for rapid patching and vulnerability management across the industry.

item.title: Hackers Allegedly Selling Exploit for Windows Remote Desktop Services 0-Day Flaw - CybersecurityNews
item.description: Threat actors are reportedly selling an exploit for a previously unknown vulnerability in Windows Remote Desktop Services (RDS), a critical remote access component used by many organizations. This zero-day flaw poses a significant risk as it could allow attackers to gain unauthorized access to systems without requiring user interaction. The availability of this exploit on the dark market increases the likelihood of widespread exploitation before Microsoft can develop and release a patch.
